coreJobStatement: Provide safe, integrated residential accommodation and healthcare services across a continuum of care levels to older adults, enabling aging in place with progressively intensive clinical support as functional needs evolve
productJobStatement: Help Facility Safety & Emergency Management to secure all emergency exit doors against unauthorized use while maintaining fire-code-compliant single-motion evacuation capability and providing real-time door status visibility to operations staff — without mains power at each door point
lPath: L7 Ecosystem > L6H-SAF Facility Safety & Emergency Management > L5 Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards

segments.name: Memory Care Unit
segments.characteristics: Residents with dementia/cognitive impairment; 60% at elopement risk; ~1–2% elope annually; 32–40% of elopements fatal within 24 h
segments.targetabilityReason: Wander management (RFID tags) dominates; exits must be secured against resident-initiated actuation while remaining fire-code compliant
segments.circumstance: Residents with dementia/cognitive impairment; 60% at elopement risk; ~1–2% elope annually; 32–40% of elopements fatal within 24 h
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — RFID wander management dominates; exits must be secured against resident-initiated actuation while remaining fire-code compliant

segments.name: Independent + Assisted Living
segments.characteristics: Functionally capable residents; no resident-initiated elopement risk; standard fire exit compliance
segments.targetabilityReason: Simpler alternatives hired: basic mechanical alarms, wired panels; lower urgency
segments.circumstance: Functionally capable residents; no resident-initiated elopement risk; standard fire exit compliance
segments.alternativesDiffer: No — simpler alternatives hired: basic mechanical alarms, wired panels; lower urgency

segments.name: Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF)
segments.characteristics: 24-h clinical care; highest CMS regulatory scrutiny; annual Life Safety survey; Immediate Jeopardy penalties >$10,000/day for elopement gaps
segments.targetabilityReason: Compliance documentation and audit-trail quality are the primary buying criteria
segments.circumstance: 24-h clinical care; highest CMS regulatory scrutiny; annual Life Safety survey; Immediate Jeopardy penalties >$10,000/day for elopement gaps
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — compliance documentation and audit-trail quality are the primary buying criteria

segments.name: Post-Acute Rehabilitation
segments.characteristics: Short-stay, high resident throughput; fire safety primary; low elopement risk
segments.targetabilityReason: Simpler exit monitoring acceptable; false-alarm tolerance higher than MC
segments.circumstance: Short-stay, high resident throughput; fire safety primary; low elopement risk
segments.alternativesDiffer: No — simpler exit monitoring acceptable; false-alarm tolerance higher than MC

segments.name: Multi-Facility Portfolio Operator
segments.characteristics: Manages many campuses; procurement is portfolio-level; needs centralized dashboard across properties
segments.targetabilityReason: Different alternatives hired: enterprise wander-management platforms vs. per-door retrofit devices
segments.circumstance: Manages many campuses; procurement is portfolio-level; needs centralized dashboard across properties
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — different alternatives hired: enterprise wander-management platforms vs. per-door retrofit devices

alternatives.name: RFID/BLE Wander Management (Stanley WanderGuard, CenTrak)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Per-resident wristband required — fails if resident removes it; requires ceiling-mounted door antennas; cannot independently satisfy NFPA 101 single-motion escape requirements; $250K–$600K+ full facility deployment; 9,000+ facility installed base

alternatives.name: Electromagnetic Delayed-Egress Locks (Assa Abloy, dormakaba)
alternatives.tradeoffs: Power-dependent — must have battery backup for fire-safe operation; NFPA 101 §7.2.1.6 limits delay to max 30 s; licensed electrician required for installation and wiring; disruptive retrofit in existing buildings

alternatives.name: IoT Emergency Exit Alarm Guards (GfS Türwächter) — this product
alternatives.tradeoffs: Alarms but does not delay exit — cannot physically prevent elopement in the way a delayed-egress lock can; EasyWave protocol not native BACnet/KNX; ~€730 per door; pre-alarm false-trigger risk in high-traffic zones

alternatives.name: Coded Keypad Exits
alternatives.tradeoffs: No monitoring capability; NFPA 101 requires single-motion escape — keypad entry adds a step and may require AHJ approval; staff must memorize codes; codes expire or are shared

alternatives.name: Manual staff monitoring / do nothing
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero hardware cost; zero false alarms; inadequate overnight/weekend coverage; CMS F-tag immediate jeopardy exposure when elopement is documented; average elopement claim = $360,000

stakeholders.who: Facility safety staff, CNAs on unit floor
CMS State Surveyor, Insurance Risk Manager

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit doors in memory care perimeter zones being excluded from alarm guard coverage, resulting in uncovered exits identified during a CMS Life Safety Code survey
Increase the ability to determine complete alarm guard coverage requirements across a multi-level care campus in a single assessment without engaging a licensed engineer or fire protection consultant
Minimize the installation and commissioning time per door for emergency exit alarm monitoring systems across a 100-bed memory care unit without requiring mains electrical work at each door
Minimize the likelihood of incorrect pre-alarm sensitivity configuration causing false alarm activations exceeding 2 incidents per door per month during normal staff passage in a memory care corridor
Increase the ability to detect a de-armed or non-functional emergency exit alarm guard before the condition is identified during a CMS inspection, with detection lag under 4 hours
Minimize the likelihood of a door alarm guard displaying armed status in the monitoring dashboard while the mechanical guard is physically disabled or in the open position
Minimize failures of emergency exit alarm activation during facility-wide power outage conditions lasting more than 8 hours

Minimize the likelihood of the exit alarm triggering due to environmental vibration or thermal cycling rather than intentional guard actuation in memory care zones where false alarms prompt immediate staff emergency response
Increase the ability to deploy monitored alarm coverage across all emergency exits in a 200-bed CCRC campus from a single centralized receiver without requiring per-door electrical infrastructure
Minimize the time from unauthorized emergency exit door activation to staff notification across all care levels and shifts, including overnight and weekend periods
Minimize variability in alarm event delivery reliability across structurally diverse building zones including basement corridors, reinforced concrete wings, and exterior stairwells
Increase the ability to view live armed/unarmed status of all emergency exits across all care levels from a single monitoring interface without logging into multiple vendor platforms
Minimize the number of IoT module battery depletion events per year that go undetected until monitoring coverage is lost on an active exit door
Minimize the total time to restore alarm guard coverage at a door following a battery replacement event, from detection of depletion to confirmed wireless re-connection

Increase the ability of a single night-shift CNA to manage simultaneous door alarm events across a 60-bed memory care floor while maintaining resident monitoring duties without requiring supervisor escalation for each event
Minimize failures in alarm guard reset capability following a valid elopement activation where the triggering resident may have applied force to the guard mechanism during the exit attempt
Increase the ability to produce audit-ready CMS Life Safety Code compliance documentation covering all emergency exit events without manual log consolidation by facility staff
Minimize the likelihood of incomplete or missing emergency exit event records at annual CMS survey time due to gaps in manual event logging across shifts

Minimize variability in emergency exit event record completeness across different shifts and staff members, ensuring documentation meets CMS survey standards regardless of which staff member was on duty
Maximize the ability to document zero unmonitored emergency exit events per quarter as evidence of proactive Life Safety compliance posture to CMS, resident families, and insurance underwriters
Minimize the risk of a CMS Immediate Jeopardy citation arising from a documented elopement through an exit door that lacked active monitoring at the time of the incident
Minimize the likelihood of on-duty memory care staff remaining unaware of a resident's emergency exit activation for more than 30 seconds after the event
